rules.lessonHeading: Unhandled promise rejections crash Node processes
rules.message: Promise .then() without .catch() — unhandled rejections crash Node 15+ processes (vercel/next.js#15049). Add .catch() or convert to async/await with try/catch.

rules.lessonHeading: Stale closure from missing effect dependencies
rules.message: useEffect called without a dependency array — stale closures will corrupt state. Add [] or list all captured variables; enable ESLint react-hooks/exhaustive-deps for full coverage (see facebook/react#29705).

rules.lessonHeading: Impure effects break in StrictMode and Concurrent Mode
rules.message: addEventListener inside useEffect must return a cleanup function calling removeEventListener — React StrictMode double-invokes effects, causing duplicate listeners

rules.lessonHeading: Server-only code leaking into client bundles
rules.message: next/headers and next/cookies are server-only — importing them in shared or component files leaks server code into the client bundle. Move to a server-only file or add `import 'server-only'` at the top.

rules.lessonHeading: Hydration mismatch from environment-dependent rendering
rules.message: Avoid typeof window checks in render body — causes hydration mismatches (vercel/next.js#44857). Move client-only rendering into useEffect.

rules.lessonHeading: Runtime crashes from missing environment variables
rules.message: Direct process.env access bypasses schema validation — validate all env vars at startup with Zod/envalid and access via the validated env object

rules.lessonHeading: Build-time vs runtime env var confusion
rules.message: Do not expose secrets via NEXT_PUBLIC_/VITE_ prefixes — these are inlined into the client bundle at build time. Read secrets from the server environment at runtime only.

rules.lessonHeading: Hardcoded localhost URLs in production code
rules.message: Hardcoded localhost/127.0.0.1 URL breaks in production — read base URLs from env vars or derive from req.headers.host

rules.lessonHeading: Type assertions (as) bypass safety checks
rules.message: Avoid 'as any' and 'as unknown as X' type assertions — use type guards or Zod schema validation at system boundaries instead

rules.lessonHeading: Destructive operations without baseline validation
rules.message: Destructive database operation without baseline validation. Guard with NODE_ENV !== 'production' or require --force flag before running (prisma/prisma#16098).

rules.lessonHeading: Unbounded payload sizes in state mechanisms
rules.message: Storing JSON-serialized data in localStorage without a byte-length check causes silent truncation — validate payload size before setItem (cookies, headers, and URL params have the same risk)

rules.lessonHeading: Empty catch blocks hide critical failures
rules.message: Empty catch block hides failures — log the error with context, rethrow, or add a comment explaining why it is safe to ignore

rules.lessonHeading: Environment-specific URL handling leaking across boundaries
rules.message: Dev-only protocol (file:// or turbopack://) in a string literal — normalize at the environment boundary using new URL() or a path resolver before use in production paths (see vercel/next.js#71489)

rules.lessonHeading: Regex/Matcher divergence between dev and prod runtimes
rules.message: Lookbehind assertions are unsupported in the Edge/V8 runtime — avoid in Next.js middleware matchers (vercel/next.js#69602)

rules.lessonHeading: Internal modules establishing cyclic dependencies on ambient type declarations
rules.message: Do not import from auto-generated ambient type declaration files (e.g., next-env.d.ts) — define explicit interface contracts within the framework instead (see vercel/next.js#34394)

rules.lessonHeading: Context flags misaligned during edge compilation
rules.message: Binary server/client split misses Edge runtime — isClient = !isServer is wrong when Edge is in play. Define isEdge explicitly and use three-way checks (see vercel/next.js#30242).

rules.lessonHeading: Style injection breaking modular chunk loading
rules.message: Do not inject <style> tags directly in JS chunks — use the bundler's CSS extraction (e.g., mini-css-extract-plugin) to avoid race conditions and duplicate style definitions (see vercel/next.js#9306)

rules.lessonHeading: Hardcoding third-party SDK dependencies into core logic
rules.message: Do not import third-party vendor SDKs directly in core routing or state management — abstract them behind provider interfaces to enable vendor replacement and testability

rules.lessonHeading: Hook rules violation inside memoization callbacks
rules.message: Do not call React hooks inside useMemo or useCallback callbacks — move hook calls to the component body and pass return values as dependencies (see facebook/react#14608)

rules.lessonHeading: Race conditions during batched state updates
rules.message: Avoid reading this.state inside setState({...}) — use setState(prev => ...) to prevent race conditions in batched updates (facebook/react#12408)

rules.lessonHeading: Serialization failures when passing complex objects to devtools
rules.message: useDebugValue called without a formatter — pass a format function as the second argument to prevent DevTools serialization crashes (facebook/react#18070)

rules.lessonHeading: Bypassing standard synthetic event systems for performance
rules.message: Avoid raw DOM addEventListener in React — use React event handlers instead. Only bypass the synthetic event system when profiling proves it necessary, and document the trade-off.

rules.lessonHeading: Memory leaks caused by calling setState on unmounted components
rules.message: async useEffect callbacks cannot return a cleanup function — move async logic into a non-async inner function and use AbortController for fetch/timeout cancellation (facebook/react#12531)

rules.lessonHeading: Assuming `setState` is synchronous
rules.message: Do not read this.state inside setState — state updates are async. Use the functional update form: this.setState(prev => ({ ...prev, key: prev.key + 1 }))

rules.lessonHeading: Evaluating defaultProps before lazy component resolution
rules.message: Do not assign defaultProps to a lazy component — the module may not be resolved yet, causing a synchronous crash. Apply defaults via destructuring in the render function instead (see facebook/react#14112).

rules.lessonHeading: Connection pooling leaks in underlying HTTP clients
rules.message: Bare PrismaClient() uses undici defaults with no connection limits or keep-alive timeouts — configure pool limits to prevent socket exhaustion under load (prisma/prisma#8831)

rules.lessonHeading: Type loss across SQL aggregate boundaries
rules.message: SQL aggregate functions return strings from the driver, not numbers. Add an explicit type: sql<number>`count(...)` or parse the result with Number() / parseInt().

rules.lessonHeading: Hardcoded component logic inside top-level layout files
rules.message: Avoid useState in layout files — extract UI logic into dedicated components; _app.tsx and layout.tsx should only compose providers and structural wrappers

rules.lessonHeading: Scaffolding scripts failing to respect existing .git states
rules.message: Check for an existing .git directory before running 'git init' — blindly initializing can corrupt an existing repo or submodule. Guard with: if (!fs.existsSync('.git')) { ... } or [ -d .git ] in shell.

rules.lessonHeading: Specificity overrides breaking AST/string serialization
rules.message: Do not append '!important' via string concatenation — use an AST-aware CSS parser to inject the modifier safely (ref: tailwindlabs/tailwindcss#14611)

rules.lessonHeading: Silent failures and "TODO" placeholders
rules.message: Do not leave hidden // TODO placeholders — throw an explicit error or render a visible UI warning for unimplemented features

rules.lessonHeading: Swallowing critical errors during SSR
rules.message: Swallowing critical errors during SSR

rules.lessonHeading: Never swallow errors with empty catch blocks
rules.message: Never swallow errors with empty catch blocks

rules.lessonHeading: Use domain-specific error classes instead of raw throw new Error
rules.message: Use domain-specific error classes instead of raw throw new Error

rules.lessonHeading: Use structured logging instead of console.log in API routes
rules.message: Use structured logging instead of console.log in API routes

rules.lessonHeading: Never build SQL queries with string concatenation
rules.message: Never build SQL queries with string concatenation

rules.lessonHeading: Never use explicit any type annotations in application code
rules.message: Never use explicit any type annotations in application code

rules.lessonHeading: Use a validated config schema instead of direct process.env access
rules.message: Use a validated config schema instead of direct process.env access
